algorithms.title: Universal Chain of Survival (2025) — ACLS Provider View
algorithms.description: The six-link universal chain with the ACLS provider's role at each link.
steps: Link 1 — Activation of emergency response: recognize arrest and activate the resuscitation team/EMS.
Link 2 — High-quality CPR: protect compression rate, depth, recoil, and fraction throughout the resuscitation.
Link 3 — Defibrillation: shock a shockable rhythm as soon as identified; resume compressions immediately.
Link 4 (ACLS provider's primary role) — Advanced resuscitation interventions: advanced airway when indicated, IV/IO access, rhythm-specific drugs and electrical therapy, identify and treat H's & T's.
Link 5 — Post-cardiac arrest care: airway/ventilation, hemodynamic support, 12-lead ECG, temperature control, consider cardiac arrest center transfer.
Link 6 — Recovery: neuroprognostication timeline, rehabilitation, survivor and family support.
System feedback loop: CQI review and Utstein-style data collection feed back into training and protocol updates.

algorithms.title: IHCA Prevention Pathway
algorithms.description: How in-hospital systems of care aim to prevent arrest before it happens.
steps: Continuous or periodic vital-sign monitoring generates an early-warning score.
Rising score triggers a rapid-response/medical emergency team activation.
Team assesses and treats the deteriorating patient at the bedside.
If arrest still occurs, the same team transitions directly into the Chain of Survival response.
